The fifth day of the Paris Olympics saw Yuxi Chen and Hongchan Quan winning gold in the women芒聙聶s synchronized diving 10-meter platform, beating the Democratic People芒聙聶s Republic of North Korea芒聙聶s duet of Jin Mi Jo and Mi Rae Kim who came second, leaving Great Britain芒聙聶s Toulson Lois and Andrea Spendolini with the bronze.

China芒聙聶s Yawen Deng also won the gold in the women's BMX freestyle beating Team USA芒聙聶s Perris Benegas and Australia芒聙聶s Natalya Diehm.

China芒聙聶s Boheng Zhang also won silver in the men芒聙聶s all-around gymnastics final alongside his teammate Ruoteng Xiao who won bronze, leaving Japan芒聙聶s Shinnosuke Oka to go home with the gold.

China芒聙聶s star swimmer Zhanle Pan won the gold in legendary fashion by breaking his world record for the men芒聙聶s 100-meter swimming freestyle with a time of 46.40. Pan had set the previous world record time at 46.80 earlier this year on February 10 in the World Aquatic Championships in Qatar芒聙聶s capital Doha.

China is now in first place in the current Olympic medal rankings at the end of day five with 9 gold medals leaving hosts France in second and Japan in third place.

Team USA also had a good day at the office, with a surprise victory at the quarter-finals of the men's tennis doubles, as Austin Kracjicek and Rajeev Ram beat Spain芒聙聶s tennis icon Rafael Nadal who had partnered up with the world芒聙聶s number one Carlos Alcaraz. They will now progress to the semi-finals.

The much-awaited basketball game between Team USA芒聙聶s star-stacked squad against exciting newcomers South Sudan, saw Lebron James lead his country to another emphatic win 103-86, progressing to the quarter-finals. The U.S. will be looking for a historic fifth straight gold medal in men's basketball.

American swimmer Katie Ledecky broke the Olympic record that she set in the 2021 Tokyo Olympics, and won a mind-boggling 12th career Olympic medal, after finishing first in the women芒聙聶s 1500-meter freestyle final. Ledecky has now tied聽 Jenny Thompson, Dara Torres, and Natalie Coughlin for the most ever medals won by an American female swimmer.

Hosts France also had a gold medal to celebrate after swimmer Leon Marchand won the Men芒聙聶s 200-meter breaststroke final setting a new Olympic record. Australia芒聙聶s Zac Stubblety-Cook won the silver and the Netherlands芒聙聶s Casper Corbeau came in third for the bronze.
